perturbation.original_text: The Stilwell Group agrees to comply with the Company's insider trading and disclosure policies, as in effect from time to time, to the same extent as if it were a director of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: The Stilwell Group agrees to comply with the Company's insider trading and disclosure policies, as in effect from time to time, except that the Stilwell Group's compliance will be reviewed annually, not continuously.
perturbation.explanation: Section 1 says The Stilwell Group agrees to comply with the Company's insider trading and disclosure policies, as in effect from time to time, to the same extent as if it were a director of the Company.' by stating the exception to the compliance review timeline contradicts the original 'to the same extent as if it were a director of the Company'. This creates a potential legal issue, since director policies are continuously being reviewed, not annually, and a potential loophole in the compliance for insider trading.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with Schneider serving on the Company's board, Schneider and other Company employees, directors, and agents may divulge nonpublic information concerning the Company and its subsidiaries to the Stilwell Group and such information may be shared among the Stilwell Group's employees and agents who have a need to know such information. The Stilwell Group expressly agrees to maintain all nonpublic information concerning the Company and its subsidiaries in confidence, unless required to disclose such information by a valid subpoena or other legal process. The Stilwell Group expressly acknowledges that federal and state securities laws may prohibit a person from purchasing or selling securities of a company, or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person is likely to purchase or sell such securities, while the first-mentioned person is in possession of material nonpublic information about such company. The Stilwell Group agrees to comply with the Company's insider trading and disclosure policies, as in effect from time to time, except that the Stilwell Group's compliance will be reviewed annually, not continuously, to the same extent as if it were a director of the Company. To the extent the nonpublic information concerning the Company and its subsidiaries received by the Stilwell Group is material, this Agreement is intended to satisfy the confidentiality agreement exclusion of Regulation FD of the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") set forth in Section 243.100(b)(2)(ii) of Regulation FD.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the compliance review timeline for The Stilwell Group, stating it will be reviewed annually instead of continuously, which contradicts the original agreement that they would comply 'to the same extent as if it were a director of the Company.' Directors' compliance is continuously reviewed, and the contradicted text repeats the changed text. This creates a direct conflict and potential loophole, making the compliance standards inconsistent within the document and leading to a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the Stilwell Group and Schneider represents and warrants to the Company that this Agreement has been duly and validly authorized (in the case of the entity members of the Stilwell Group), executed and delivered by them, and is a valid and binding agreement enforceable against them in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Stilwell Group and Schneider represents and warrants to the Company that this Agreement has been duly and validly authorized (in the case of the entity members of the Stilwell Group), executed and delivered by them, and is a valid and binding agreement enforceable against them in accordance with its terms, except that the enforceability is limited to monetary damages, not specific performance.
perturbation.explanation: Section 2 states the agreement is 'enforceable against them in accordance with its terms.' By adding 'except that the enforceability is limited to monetary damages, not specific performance,' directly contradicts the 'enforceable against them in accordance with its terms' part, because it removes remedies that would otherwise be available.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the Stilwell Group and Schneider represents and warrants to the Company that this Agreement has been duly and validly authorized (in the case of the entity members of the Stilwell Group), executed and delivered by them, and is a valid and binding agreement enforceable against them in accordance with its terms, except that the enforceability is limited to monetary damages, not specific performance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception, 'except that the enforceability is limited to monetary damages, not specific performance,' to the original statement that the agreement is 'enforceable against them in accordance with its terms.' This creates a direct conflict because the original text implies all remedies are available, while the changed text restricts the available remedies. Since the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, there is a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The Stilwell Group acknowledges that with regard to its obligations to maintain the confidentiality of nonpublic information of the Company and its subsidiaries, monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and that, in addition to all other remedies, the Company may be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for such breach, and in conjunction therewith the Company shall not be required to post any bond.
perturbation.changed_text: The Stilwell Group acknowledges that with regard to its obligations to maintain the confidentiality of nonpublic information of the Company and its subsidiaries, monetary damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, and the Company shall not be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for such breach, and the Company shall be required to post a bond.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original text of Section 4. The original text states monetary damages might not be sufficient and allows for seeking specific performance. The modified version explicitly states monetary damages are the *sole* remedy and prohibits seeking specific performance, also adding the need for the company to post a bond to seek relief, if monetary damages do not work. These terms are directly opposite to each other.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Stilwell Group acknowledges that with regard to its obligations to maintain the confidentiality of nonpublic information of the Company and its subsidiaries, monetary damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement, and the Company shall not be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for such breach, and the Company shall be required to post a bond.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. It initially states that monetary damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy and that the Company shall not be entitled to seek specific performance, and then it adds that the Company shall be required to post a bond. This creates an internal contradiction within the changed text because the need for a bond implies that monetary damages might not be sufficient, directly conflicting with the first part of the text. In this instance, the changed text and contradicted text are in the same location; it is a direct in-text contradiction as defined in structural flaws.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto pertaining to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations and discussions of the parties in connection therewith not referred to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto pertaining to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations and discussions of the parties in connection therewith not referred to herein, except for a separate, unwritten agreement regarding director's fees for Schneider.
perturbation.explanation: Section 5 is an integration clause stating the agreement is the entire agreement. Adding 'except for a separate, unwritten agreement...' completely undermines this clause, creating uncertainty as to what agreements actually govern the parties' relationship and making it unclear if any aspect of the unwritten agreement is considered relevant or if that agreement exist, making the document not represent the full understanding between the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto pertaining to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations and discussions of the parties in connection therewith not referred to herein, except for a separate, unwritten agreement regarding director's fees for Schneider.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement constitutes the entire agreement. The changed text introduces an exception to this, stating that there is a separate, unwritten agreement regarding director's fees for Schneider. This directly contradicts the integration clause, creating a clear and direct conflict within the document. The Contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text in this scenario, and the change causes it to contradict itself.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified, amended, altered or supplemented except upon the execution and delivery of a written agreement executed by all of the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be modified, amended, altered or supplemented except upon the execution and delivery of a written agreement executed by all of the parties hereto, or by unanimous verbal consent of all parties recorded in meeting minutes.
perturbation.explanation: Section 9 requires modifications to be in writing. Allowing verbal modifications by unanimous consent, that are being recorded in meeting minutes, contradicts this requirement and it introduces ambiguity and potential disputes about what modifications were actually agreed upon, potentially making it difficult to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may not be modified, amended, altered or supplemented except upon the execution and delivery of a written agreement executed by all of the parties hereto, or by unanimous verbal consent of all parties recorded in meeting minutes.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that modifications must be in writing. The changed text introduces an exception allowing verbal modifications recorded in meeting minutes. This directly contradicts the initial requirement for written modifications, creating a structural flaw and ambiguity, as it becomes unclear whether modifications must be written or can be verbal. The Perturbation Explanation further highlights this contradiction.
